description:
Automated translation (refer to source for accuracy)

🔄 Content Updated (Published: June 30, 2026)

Hanno City Museum will hold a special exhibition "Hanno and the Showa Era—The City's Birth and Evolving Life" from July 19 (Sunday) to August 30 (Sunday), 2026 (Reiwa 8). Museum hours: 9:30 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. Closed Mondays (except July 20), July 21 (Tuesday), July 24 (Friday), August 12 (Wednesday), and August 28 (Friday).

The exhibition showcases the history of Hanno's area through successive mergers and the changes in people's daily life through household items and appliances, using materials from the museum's collection.

【Exhibition Explanations by Curators】
(Same content all dates)
• July 30 (Thursday): 10:00–10:30 a.m. and 3:00–3:30 p.m.
• August 16 (Sunday): 10:00–10:30 a.m. and 3:00–3:30 p.m.
• August 23 (Sunday): 10:00–10:30 a.m. and 3:00–3:30 p.m.

【Twilight Museum】
(Extended hours until 7:00 p.m., exhibition explanation from 6:00 p.m. for about 30 minutes)
• August 11 (Tuesday, national holiday)
• August 29 (Saturday)

【Related Lecture】
"Postcards Tell Hanno's 100 Years of Showa"
Date: August 1 (Saturday), 2026, 1:00–4:00 p.m.
Location: Hanno City Museum Learning and Training Room
Instructor: Hiroyuki Kato (regional researcher)
Registration: Apply online or by phone to the museum. Limited capacity, first come, first served.

【Workshop】
Scheduled for August 22 (Saturday), 2026. Details will be announced as they are confirmed.

Source (Japanese): https://www.city.hanno.lg.jp/soshikikarasagasu/kyoikubu/hakubutsukan/tenji_moyooshimono/13451.html
summary: ℹ️ Special Exhibition: "Hanno and the Showa Era—The City's Birth and Evolving Life" (July 19 – August 30, 2026)
